_metadata.description: Robots in Disguise (2001) characters — Car Robots / RiD 2001 TV series (39 episodes)

characters.name: Optimus Prime
characters.notes: Autobot leader. Transforms into a fire truck. Can combine with Ultra Magnus to form Omega Prime. Known as Fire Convoy in Japan

characters.name: Side Burn
characters.notes: Youngest of the Autobot Brothers. Speed-obsessed with a compulsive attraction to red sports cars. Known as Speedbreaker in Japan

characters.name: Prowl
characters.notes: Middle brother of the Autobot Brothers. Strict, by-the-book law enforcement officer. Known as Mach Alert in Japan

characters.name: X-Brawn
characters.notes: Eldest of the Autobot Brothers. Toughest and most adventurous, a rugged outdoorsman. Known as Wildride in Japan

characters.name: T-AI
characters.notes: Autobot artificial intelligence coordinator. Manifests as a holographic young woman. Manages the Global Space Bridge and coordinates Autobot forces

characters.name: Koji Onishi
characters.notes: Human boy and primary Autobot ally. Son of Dr. Kenneth Onishi. Can command Fortress Maximus

characters.name: Megatron
characters.alt_mode: Six modes: bat, two-headed dragon, jet, car, hand, robot
characters.notes: Predacon leader. Six-changer. Captures Dr. Onishi for his knowledge of ancient ruins. Later upgrades to Galvatron. Known as Gigatron in Japan

characters.name: Sky-Byte
characters.notes: Predacon sub-commander and fan-favorite character. Aspiring poet with a love of haiku. Desperate for Megatron's approval. Commands the Predacon trio. Known as Gelshark in Japan

characters.name: Dark Scream
characters.notes: Predacon trio member under Sky-Byte's command. Transforms into a techno-organic flying squirrel. Provides aerial recon

characters.name: Gas Skunk
characters.notes: Predacon trio member under Sky-Byte's command. Smartest of the trio, skilled inventor. Can emit corrosive smoke clouds

characters.name: Slapper
characters.notes: Predacon trio member under Sky-Byte's command. His tongue extends 42 feet as a flail weapon

characters.name: Scourge
characters.notes: Decepticon leader and dark clone of Optimus Prime. Created from an Autobot protoform. Wields the Sword of Fury. Known as Black Convoy in Japan

characters.name: Mega-Octane
characters.alt_mode: Flatbed military truck with twin-barreled cannon
characters.notes: Decepticon Commando and practical leader of the team. Controls the combined form Ruination. Known as Dolrailer in Japan

characters.name: Ro-Tor
characters.notes: Decepticon Commando. Known as Hepter in Japan

characters.name: Movor
characters.notes: Decepticon Commando. Can achieve orbit under his own power. Known as Shuttler in Japan

characters.name: Armorhide
characters.notes: Decepticon Commando. Known as Dangar in Japan

characters.name: Rollbar
characters.notes: Decepticon Commando. Practices Crystalocution martial arts. Known as Greejeeber in Japan

characters.name: Ruination
characters.notes: Decepticon combiner formed from the five Commandos. Controlled by Mega-Octane. Can reconfigure limbs for different mission modes. Known as Valdigus in Japan

characters.name: Ultra Magnus
characters.notes: Optimus Prime's brother. Bitter about being passed over for the Matrix. Reluctant ally who combines with Optimus to form Omega Prime. Known as God Magnus in Japan

characters.name: Railspike
characters.notes: Leader of Team Bullet Train. Experienced battle-tested veteran. Known as J-Five in Japan

characters.name: Rapid Run
characters.alt_mode: 700 Series Hikari Rail Star bullet train
characters.notes: Team Bullet Train member. Physically strongest of the team. Can generate force fields. Known as J-Seven in Japan

characters.name: Midnight Express
characters.notes: Team Bullet Train member. Fussy and easily flustered. Shows empathy toward non-sentient trains. Known as J-Four in Japan

characters.name: Rail Racer
characters.notes: Autobot combiner formed from Team Bullet Train. Sometimes refers to itself as 'we'. Known as JRX in Japan

characters.name: Wedge
characters.notes: Leader of the Build Team. Young 'boy genius' and chief architect of the Global Space Bridge. Known as Build Boy in Japan

characters.name: Grimlock
characters.notes: Build Team second-in-command. NOT the G1 Dinobot — this is a construction vehicle Transformer. Known as Build Hurricane in Japan

characters.name: Heavy Load
characters.notes: Build Team member. Despite his bulk, has studied martial arts. Mentor to team leader Wedge. Known as Build Typhoon in Japan

characters.name: Hightower
characters.notes: Build Team member and best marksman. Acts as bodyguard and advisor to Wedge. Known as Build Cyclone in Japan

characters.name: Landfill
characters.notes: Autobot combiner formed from the Build Team. Controlled by Wedge. Known as Build King in Japan

characters.name: Omega Prime
characters.notes: Autobot combined form of Optimus Prime and Ultra Magnus. More powerful than the sum of his parts. Wields the Matrix Blade

characters.name: Fortress Maximus
characters.notes: Colossal Autobot guardian hidden on Earth. 350 meters tall. Not independently sentient — controlled via Cerebros. Known as Brave Maximus in Japan

characters.name: Cerebros
characters.notes: Mindless drone that serves as the key to controlling Fortress Maximus. Transforms into the Emissary's head. Known as Plasma in Japan

characters.name: Hot Shot
characters.notes: Leader of the Spychangers. Has pyrokinetic abilities. Smaller than average Autobots, specializing in stealth and covert operations

characters.name: R.E.V.
characters.notes: Spychanger team member. Specializes in stealth missions and covert espionage

characters.name: Crosswise
characters.notes: Spychanger team member. Specializes in stealth missions and covert espionage

characters.name: W.A.R.S.
characters.notes: Spychanger team member. Name stands for 'Wicked Attack Recon Sportscar'

characters.name: Ironhide
characters.notes: Spychanger team member. NOT the G1 Autobot — different character. Known as Ox in Japan

characters.name: Mirage
characters.notes: Spychanger team member. NOT the G1 Autobot — different character. Known as Counter Arrow in Japan

characters.name: Skid-Z
characters.notes: Independent Autobot racer. Obsessed with racing after scanning the car of deceased driver Auggie Cahnay. Known as Indy Heat in Japan

characters.name: Tow-Line
characters.notes: Independent Autobot. Obsessively enforces traffic laws, towing illegally parked vehicles. Known as Wrecker Hook in Japan

characters.name: Galvatron
characters.alt_mode: Ten modes including dragon, pteranodon, hydrofoil, elephant, and others
characters.notes: Upgraded form of Megatron after absorbing energy from the Orb of Sigma. Ten-changer with vastly enhanced power. Known as Devil Gigatron in Japan

characters.name: Dr. Kenneth Onishi
characters.notes: Archaeologist and energy expert. Father of Koji. Kidnapped by Megatron for his knowledge of ancient ruins

characters.name: Kelly
characters.notes: Recurring comic relief character with extraordinarily bad luck. Constantly has her red sports cars destroyed in Transformer battles
